title: AI Reviewer Feedback Triage
context: GrantLayer is publicly readable on GitHub at https://github.com/Discodone/grantlayer.git in a Developer Preview / Controlled Pilot posture following GL-176 through GL-185. GL-185 opened the first reviewer feedback window and defined the feedback capture model. No real human reviewer outreach has been sent yet. The user gathered three AI-simulated external reviewer reports. GL-186 triages these reports, normalizes findings into the GL-183 category/severity model, identifies repeated themes, separates blockers from non-blocking polish, and recommends next concrete GL issues.
scope: docs/test/artifact only — no backend, no API, no DB, no runtime changes

input_reviews.title: Codex Backend/DX Review
positive_signals: GrantLayer is understandable as a local-first verification/audit/compliance layer for agentic grant workflows
First verifiable output is discoverable and reproducible
First verifiable output ran successfully and matched committed reference JSON
Safety caveats are explicit and appropriately bounded
major_concerns: README says 'no installation required' but backend quickstart needs venv and requirements (GL-REV-01, medium)
Port inconsistency: backend quickstart references 8765, integration example uses 8000 (GL-REV-02, low)
README 'Source of truth | Internal Forgejo' wording is confusing in a public repo (GL-REV-03, low)

findings.summary: README 'no installation required' conflicts with backend quickstart venv/requirements setup
Port inconsistency: backend quickstart 8765 vs integration example 8000
Source of truth Forgejo wording confusing in a public repo
Next example requested: grant creation, audit trail query, verifiable evidence export
README lacks a clear 'choose your path' section

input_reviews.title: Kimi K2.6 Agent/OSS Review
input_reviews.persona: AI-agent workflow developer and first-time open-source reviewer
positive_signals: README is clear enough to understand GrantLayer quickly
AGENTS.md, llms.txt, and llms-full.txt are unusually strong and useful for agent onboarding
First verifiable output is easy to locate and runs deterministically
major_concerns: ten_minute_quickstart.md still uses placeholder/future public GitHub clone language (GL-REV-KIMI-01, medium)
agent_quickstart.md says 'approved internal source' for cloning, misleading in a public repo (GL-REV-KIMI-02, medium)
AGENTS.md and llms-full.txt contain stale 'Public GitHub release not performed' language (GL-REV-KIMI-03, low)
llms-full.txt Next Planned Issues table is stale (GL-REV-KIMI-04, low)
README clone command and cd path slightly inconsistent (GL-REV-KIMI-05, low)

findings.summary: ten_minute_quickstart.md uses placeholder/future public GitHub clone language
agent_quickstart.md refers to 'approved internal source' for cloning
AGENTS.md and llms-full.txt stale public-release language
llms-full.txt Next Planned Issues table is stale
README clone command and cd directory path slightly inconsistent
Clarify SDK/API status: local import only vs published package

input_reviews.title: Security/Product-Readiness Review
input_reviews.persona: security-oriented senior technical reviewer with product-readiness awareness
positive_signals: GrantLayer is a clearly bounded Developer Preview / Controlled Pilot
First verifiable output is cryptographically reproducible and matches committed reference output exactly
Safety caveats are honest and repeated
No broken links among checked files
No real secrets or customer data found in the review
major_concerns: README test count claim is immediately falsifiable — actual runs show thousands of tests and known failures (F-001, high)
CONTRIBUTING.md states public GitHub release not performed but repository is already public (F-002, high)
README Suggested repository metadata speaks hypothetically about public publication approval (F-003, medium)
GL-155 described as both planned and completed in different places (F-004, medium)
ENABLE_DEMO_ENDPOINTS=true combined with non-localhost host may expose unauthenticated tamper endpoint — kept high-level only (F-005, medium, security-concern)
sourceOfTruth: internal-forgejo in public JSON files confuses external reviewers (F-006, low)

findings.summary: README test count is stale and immediately falsifiable
CONTRIBUTING.md states public release not performed but repo is already public
README metadata section uses hypothetical public publication framing
GL-155 described as both planned and completed in different places
ENABLE_DEMO_ENDPOINTS=true with non-localhost host may expose unauthenticated tamper endpoint — high-level only, routed to GitHub Security Advisories or later hardening issue
sourceOfTruth: internal-forgejo in public JSON files confuses external reviewers

normalized_findings.summary: README test count is stale and immediately falsifiable
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: README claims '1130 tests, 3 skipped, 0 failures'. Actual suites show thousands of tests and some known failures. A developer who runs the test suite will immediately see a contradiction.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Update the test count claim to reflect reality, or replace with a dynamic instruction such as 'run scripts/run-full-backend-suite.sh and check output'.

normalized_findings.summary: CONTRIBUTING.md states public GitHub release not performed but repository is already public
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: CONTRIBUTING.md contains language requiring approval for public release and stating the release has not been performed. The repository is already publicly accessible at https://github.com/Discodone/grantlayer.git.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Update CONTRIBUTING.md to reflect actual public state. Remove or rewrite the approval-required / release-not-performed language.

normalized_findings.summary: Clone instructions in quickstart docs use inconsistent or hypothetical language instead of actual public URL
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: ten_minute_quickstart.md uses placeholder/future public clone language. README clone command and cd directory name are slightly inconsistent. A developer following the quickstart encounters friction at the first step.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Update all clone commands to use the actual public repository URL. Align directory names in README clone example with actual checkout directory.

normalized_findings.summary: agent_quickstart.md refers to 'approved internal source' for cloning
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: agent_quickstart.md instructs cloning from an 'approved internal source'. This is misleading to external developers or AI agents reading the public repository.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Update agent_quickstart.md to use the actual public repository URL or add a clear explanation of the public vs internal source relationship.

normalized_findings.summary: AGENTS.md and llms-full.txt contain stale public-release language and stale Next Planned Issues table
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: AGENTS.md contains 'Public GitHub release not performed' / 'formal visibility decision pending' language. llms-full.txt Next Planned Issues table does not reflect current state post GL-176 through GL-185.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Update AGENTS.md and llms-full.txt to reflect actual public state. Update or remove stale Next Planned Issues section.

normalized_findings.summary: README Suggested repository metadata section uses hypothetical public publication framing
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: The README metadata section still speaks as if public publication is a future hypothetical. The repository is already public.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Rewrite or remove the hypothetical framing. Update metadata section to reflect actual public state.

normalized_findings.summary: GL-155 described as both planned and completed in different places
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: One location describes GL-155 as a planned future issue while another location describes it as completed and added. Contradiction creates confusion about the actual state of examples.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Audit all references to GL-155 across docs and resolve to a single consistent description of its status.

normalized_findings.summary: Internal Forgejo / sourceOfTruth: internal-forgejo appears in public-facing docs and JSON files
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: Multiple public-facing files reference the internal Forgejo mirror without explanation. External reviewers not aware of the internal setup are confused by this wording.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Exclude these references from public snapshot exports or add a brief explanation of the Forgejo/GitHub mirror relationship in README or a dedicated doc.

normalized_findings.summary: Port inconsistency between backend quickstart (8765) and integration example (8000)
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: Developer following backend quickstart expects port 8765 but integration example uses 8000. Minor friction but immediately visible.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Standardize port references across quickstart docs and integration examples, or add a clear note explaining the difference.

normalized_findings.summary: README clone command and cd directory path are slightly inconsistent
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: The cd directory name suggested in README does not exactly match the directory created by the clone command. Minor but creates friction on first use.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Align README clone command example with the actual checkout directory name.

normalized_findings.evidence_summary: Currently a developer must manually compare the first verifiable output against the committed reference JSON. A small helper script would improve the DX and reduce friction for new developers and AI agents.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Create a small script or test in scripts/ or examples/ that runs the first verifiable output and compares against committed reference output.

normalized_findings.summary: Add a second runnable example covering grant creation, audit trail query, and verifiable evidence export
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: Currently only one verifiable example is available. A second example demonstrating the core grant lifecycle would significantly improve the developer preview's coverage and usefulness.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Create a second runnable public example showing: grant creation, audit trail query, and verifiable evidence bundle export. Docs/examples only, no backend/src changes.

normalized_findings.summary: ENABLE_DEMO_ENDPOINTS=true with non-localhost host binding may expose unauthenticated tamper endpoint — high-level only
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: Configuration-level risk noted by security reviewer. Kept at description level only. No exploit details included in this document.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Create GL-190 as a separate higher-care backend hardening issue to add a startup warning or validation guard. Route security-sensitive details to GitHub Security Advisories per SECURITY.md.
normalized_findings.security_handling_note: Details kept high-level only. No exploit details. Routed to GitHub Security Advisories or GL-190 hardening issue.

normalized_findings.summary: README lacks a clear 'choose your path' section to guide developers to the right starting point
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: Developers arriving at the README face multiple entry points without clear guidance on which path to take. A short 'choose your path' section would reduce initial friction.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Add a short 'choose your path' section near the top of README guiding developers to: first verifiable output, backend quickstart, or agent integration.

normalized_findings.summary: SDK/API status not explicit: local import only vs published package
normalized_findings.evidence_summary: It is not clear from public docs whether the SDK is only available as a local import from the repository or whether a published package is planned. Agent builders and developers need this clarified.
normalized_findings.recommendation: Add a brief SDK/API status section clarifying that current state is local import only, and noting any plans for a published package.

repeated_themes.title: Stale public-state docs
repeated_themes.description: All three reviewers independently flagged that various docs still reflect a pre-publication internal state. Affected files include README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, AGENTS.md, llms-full.txt, ten_minute_quickstart.md, and agent_quickstart.md.

repeated_themes.title: First verifiable output works well
repeated_themes.description: All three reviewers confirmed the first verifiable output ran successfully, deterministically, and matched the committed reference JSON. Strong positive signal.

repeated_themes.title: Quickstart / clone-path inconsistency
repeated_themes.description: Both Codex and Kimi flagged inconsistent or hypothetical clone commands and directory names in quickstart docs.

repeated_themes.title: Agent docs strong but stale in places
repeated_themes.description: Kimi noted AGENTS.md, llms.txt, and llms-full.txt are unusually strong for agent onboarding but contain stale public-state language.

repeated_themes.title: Test count / status claims need cleanup
repeated_themes.description: Security reviewer flagged the stale test count in README as immediately falsifiable and trust-damaging.

repeated_themes.title: Verify-first-output helper script requested
repeated_themes.description: Kimi requested a small helper script to run and compare the first verifiable output in one step.

repeated_themes.title: Second runnable example needed
repeated_themes.description: Codex requested a second example covering grant creation, audit trail query, and verifiable evidence export.

repeated_themes.title: Demo endpoint safety hardening warrants separate issue
repeated_themes.description: Security reviewer flagged a configuration-level risk around the demo tamper endpoint. Should be a separate higher-care issue (GL-190), not bundled with docs cleanup.

priority_follow_up_plan.title: Public Docs Post-Public Stale Claim Cleanup
expected_scope: possibly public metadata JSON files containing sourceOfTruth: internal-forgejo
expected_outcomes: Remove or update stale test count claim
Update CONTRIBUTING.md to reflect actual public state
Update clone commands to real public URL
Update agent-facing state in AGENTS.md and llms-full.txt
Remove hypothetical framing from README metadata section
Align README clone command with cd directory name
Optionally address port inconsistency and Forgejo wording
explicit_non_goals: No GitHub push during this issue

priority_follow_up_plan.title: First Output Verify Helper Script
expected_scope: Small script or test helper in scripts/ or examples/ that runs the first verifiable output example and compares against committed reference JSON

priority_follow_up_plan.title: Second Runnable Example / Grant Lifecycle Evidence Bundle
expected_scope: New public example showing grant creation, audit trail query, and verifiable evidence export

priority_follow_up_plan.title: Demo Endpoint Safety Guard / Startup Warning
priority_follow_up_plan.recommended_model: Claude Code (Sonnet or Opus for security judgment)
expected_scope: Backend startup validation or warning when ENABLE_DEMO_ENDPOINTS=true and host is not localhost
Docs update to SECURITY.md or security_boundaries.md
priority_follow_up_plan.important_note: This involves backend/src changes and requires higher review care. Do not bundle with GL-187 docs cleanup.
explicit_non_goals: No exploit details in public artifacts
Security-sensitive details via GitHub Security Advisories only

immediate_next_issue.title: Public Docs Post-Public Stale Claim Cleanup
immediate_next_issue.rationale: Three independent reviewers flagged stale public-state docs, broken clone instructions, and trust-damaging test-count/status claims. NF-001 and NF-002 are both high-severity and immediately falsifiable by any external developer who runs the test suite or reads CONTRIBUTING.md. These are more urgent than adding new features or helper scripts because they undermine the basic trust signal that the developer preview is meant to build.
explicit_non_goals: No GitHub push during this issue

security_sensitive_handling.summary: Configuration-level risk around ENABLE_DEMO_ENDPOINTS=true with non-localhost host binding
security_sensitive_handling.handling: Kept at description level only. No exploit details, no proof-of-concept, no specific attack paths included in this document or any artifact in this issue.
security_sensitive_handling.recommended_action: Create GL-190 as a separate higher-care backend hardening issue. Route security-sensitive details to GitHub Security Advisories per SECURITY.md.
security_sensitive_handling.routed_to: GitHub Security Advisories (per SECURITY.md) or GL-190 hardening issue

non_goals: Create GitHub issues or labels via API
Assert multi-tenant or workspace isolation as a completed feature
